title: andropogon decipiens
intro: Andropogon decipiens, also known as East Indian sweetgrass, is a type of perennial grass native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, including India and China. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of East Indian sweetgrass include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. East Indian sweetgrass can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, decoctions, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
